vocabulary.context: He would delight them equally by his anecdotes of witchcraft, and of the direful omens and portentous sights and sounds in the air, which prevailed in the earlier times of Connecticut; and would frighten them woefully with speculations upon comets and shooting stars; and with the alarming fact 21.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他会用他的巫术轶事以及康涅狄格州早期盛行的可怕预兆、不祥的景象和空气中的声音来让他们同样高兴。并会用对彗星和流星的猜测来吓坏他们；以及令人震惊的事实21

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但是，与这些严肃、有信誉、虔诚的人、60 61教会的长老、贞洁的女士和露水的处女不敬地交往的，有生活放荡的男人和名声大噪的女人，还有犯有卑鄙和肮脏恶习的可怜虫，甚至涉嫌犯有可怕的罪行

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在这些令人愉快和值得赞扬的沉思中，古德曼·布朗听到了路边马蹄声，并认为最好将自己隐藏在森林边缘，意识到把他带到这里的罪恶目的，尽管现在很高兴地离开了它

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这让他更加烦恼，因为他可以发誓，如果有可能发生这样的事情，他认出了牧师和执事古金的声音，他们安静地慢跑着，就像他们 40 41 在接受某种任命或教会会议时习惯做的那样

vocabulary.definition: n. 亵渎者, 辱骂者

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当牧师在讲坛上，手放在打开的《圣经》上，以有力和热情的口才，讲述我们宗教的神圣真理、圣徒般的生活和胜利的死亡，以及未来难以言喻的幸福或痛苦时，古德曼·布朗的脸色变得苍白，担心屋顶会轰然倒塌，砸在灰色的亵渎者和他的听众身上

vocabulary.context: Besides, the few miracles attributed to the angel showed a certain mental disorder, like the blind man who didn't recover his sight but grew three new teeth, or the paralytic who didn't get to walk but almost won the lottery, and the leper whose sores sprouted sunflowers.

vocabulary.definition: n. 心脏的跳动, 热情, 柔情, (美)(非正式)情人
vocabulary.context: My 10th-grade heartthrob was the daughter of a fearsome steelworker who struck terror into the hearts of 15-year-old boys.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 我十年级时的万人迷是一位可怕的钢铁工人的女儿，她让 15 岁男孩心生恐惧

vocabulary.definition: adj. 每日的, 每日发作的, 司空见惯的, 平凡的；n. 日发热
vocabulary.context: As the writer Michael Lewis observed in his book "Next: The Future Just Happened," children see the Net as a transformational device that lets them discard quotidian identities for more glamorous ones.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如作家迈克尔·刘易斯在他的著作《下一步：未来刚刚发生》中所观察到的那样，孩子们将网络视为一种变革性的工具，可以让他们抛弃平凡的身份，转而追求更迷人的身份

vocabulary.definition: adj. 阳光照射不到的, 阴暗的
vocabulary.context: No group has embraced this socially impoverishing trade-off more enthusiastically than adolescents, many of whom spend most of their free hours cruising the Net in sunless rooms.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有哪个群体比青少年更热情地接受这种使社会贫困的权衡，他们中的许多人将大部分空闲时间花在没有阳光的房间里上网

vocabulary.definition: adj. 社交的, 群居的
vocabulary.context: Studies show that gregarious, well-connected people actually lost friends, and experienced symptoms of loneliness and depression, after joining discussion groups and other activities.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 研究表明，善于交际、人脉广泛的人在参加讨论小组和其他活动后实际上会失去朋友，并出现孤独和抑郁的症状

vocabulary.definition: adj. 奥妙的, 密封的, 不透气的, 不漏气的, 与外界隔绝的；[计] 全密封的
vocabulary.context: This hermetic existence has left many of these teenagers with nonexistent social skills - a point widely noted in stories about the computer geeks who rose to prominence in the early days of Silicon Valley.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种与世隔绝的生活让许多青少年缺乏社交技能--这一点在硅谷早期崭露头角的计算机极客的故事中被广泛提及

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 包抄, 迂回, 智胜
vocabulary.context: Faced with the same challenge today, however, I would probably pass on meeting the girl's father - and outflank him on the Internet.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，今天面对同样的挑战，我可能会放弃与女孩父亲的会面，并在互联网上包抄他

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 唆使, 怂恿, 引诱, 诱奸；[法] 勾引, 诱惑, 诱奸
vocabulary.context: People who communicated with disembodied strangers online found the experience empty and emotionally frustrating but were nonetheless seduced by the novelty of the new medium.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在网上与无形的陌生人交流的人们发现这种体验空虚且情感上令人沮丧，但仍然被新媒体的新颖性所吸引

vocabulary.definition: adj. 富有魅力的, 迷人的
vocabulary.context: As the writer Michael Lewis observed in his book "Next: The Future Just Happened," children see the Net as a transformational device that lets them discard quotidian identities for more glamorous ones.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如作家迈克尔·刘易斯在他的著作《下一步：未来刚刚发生》中所观察到的那样，孩子们将网络视为一种变革性的工具，可以让他们抛弃平凡的身份，转而追求更迷人的身份

vocabulary.definition: n. 分光镜；[化] 分光镜
vocabulary.context: And in a hundred observatories there had been suppressed excitement, rising almost to shouting pitch, as the two remote bodies had rushed together; and a hurrying to and fro, to gather photographic apparatus and spectroscope, and this appliance and that, to record this novel astonishing sight, the destruction of a world.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当两个遥远的天体冲到一起时，一百个天文台都压抑着兴奋，几乎达到了喊叫的程度；匆匆忙忙地来回收集摄影器材和分光镜，以及这个和那个，记录这个新奇的惊人景象，一个世界的毁灭

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不可触知的, 不能感知的, 难以理解的；[医] 不可接触的, 极细微的
vocabulary.context: The sun with its specks of planets, its dust of planetoids, and its impalpable comets, swims in a vacant immensity that almost defeats the imagination.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 太阳及其行星的斑点、小行星的尘埃以及难以捉摸的彗星，在几乎无法想象的空旷无垠中游动

vocabulary.definition: adv. 无节制地, 失禁地, 不能自制地
vocabulary.context: Neptune it was, had been struck, fairly and squarely, by the strange planet from outer space and the heat of the concussion had incontinently turned two solid globes into one vast mass of incandescence.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它是海王星，被这颗来自外太空的奇怪行星公平而直接地撞击，震荡的热量不禁将两个固体球体变成了一团巨大的白炽光

vocabulary.definition: adj. 折射的, 有折射力的；[医] 折射的, 屈光的
vocabulary.context: It was perceptibly larger; in the clear refractive sky of the tropics it seemed as if it were nearly a quarter the size of the moon.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 它明显更大；在热带地区清澈的折射天空中，它看起来几乎有月亮的四分之一大小

vocabulary.definition: n. 稳健, 坚定, 不变
vocabulary.context: The star grew-it grew with a terrible steadiness hour after hour, a little larger each hour, a little nearer the midnight zenith, and brighter and brighter, until it had turned night into a second day.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 星星长大了--它一小时又一小时地以可怕的稳定性长大，每小时都变大一点，离午夜天顶更近一点，而且越来越亮，直到把黑夜变成第二天

vocabulary.definition: adj. 无实体的, 不坚固的, 浅薄的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 并且，拯救了几颗比最薄弱的火焰更虚幻的彗星，无论人类知识是否曾经跨越过这个空间鸿沟，直到二十世纪初这个奇怪的流浪者出现。 【06】还有，白眼，同行

vocabulary.definition: n. 麻木不仁
vocabulary.context: Yet all that we have received in return is just another smooth exercise in political manipulation, deceit and deception, callousness and indifference to our individual problems, and the disgusting playing of divisive politics, pitting the young against the old, labor against management, North against South, blacks against whites.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 然而，我们得到的回报只是政治操纵、欺骗与欺骗、对我们个人问题的冷漠和冷漠，以及令人厌恶的分裂政治游戏，使年轻人与老年人、劳工与管理层、北方与南方、黑人与白人对立

vocabulary.definition: n. 集会, 市场

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 因此，如果任何两个人渴望同样的东西，但他们无法同时享受，他们就会成为敌人；并且，在达到目的的过程中，主要是为了保护自己，有时只是为了取悦，而努力摧毁或征服彼此

vocabulary.context: Furthermore, no distinction shall be made on the basis of the political, jurisdictional or international status of the country or territory to which a person belongs, whether it be independent, trust, non-self-governing or under any other limitation of sovereignty.

vocabulary.definition: n. 寡妇身分, 守寡
vocabulary.context: Everyone has the right to a standard of living adequate for the health and well-being of himself and of his family, including food, clothing, housing and medical care and necessary social services, and the right to security in the event of unemployment, sickness, disability, widowhood, old age or other lack of livelihood in circumstances beyond his control.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 人人有权享有足以维持其本人及其家人的健康和福祉的生活水平，包括食物、衣服、住房和医疗保健以及必要的社会服务，并有权在失业、疾病、残疾、丧偶、年老或其他无法控制的情况下缺乏生计时获得保障

vocabulary.definition: n. 可敬, 有社会地位, 高尚, 体面, 习俗

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 1999年下台时，曼德拉尽管尽了最大努力，却留下了一个重大问题：解决白人在种族隔离之前和种族隔离期间频繁暴力剥夺土地的遗留问题

vocabulary.definition: adj. 婚外的

Next, the Afrikaner-dominated parliament prohibited extra-marital sex between white people and people of other races, and it legalized the segregation of public premises, vehicles, facilities and services.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...法案"禁止"欧洲人"（白人）和"非欧洲人"之间的婚姻。接下来，南非荷兰语主导的议会禁止白人和其他种族的人之间的婚外性行为，并将公共场所、车辆、设施和服务的隔离合法化

vocabulary.definition: n. 选民, 顾客, 读者；[法] 选区, 全体选民, 选区内的选民

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 面对全球日益孤立和本国白人选民日益强烈的反对，南非政府别无选择，只能屈服

vocabulary.context: The British, in particular, denounced apartheid as an unacceptable system of governance; United Nations passed resolutions excoriating the South African government; a Norwegian committee awarded the 1960 Nobel Peace Prize to Albert Luthuli, the president of ANC; and South Africa was not allowed to participate in the Olympics.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 英国人尤其谴责种族隔离是一种不可接受的治理制度。联合国通过决议谴责南非政府；挪威的一个委员会将 1960 年诺贝尔和平奖授予非国大主席阿尔伯特·卢图利 (Albert Luthuli)；南非则不被允许参加奥运会

vocabulary.definition: n. 大主教

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他的内阁中包括了所有种族的南非人；敦促南非荷兰语人和其他白人留在该国；并与著名大主教德斯蒙德·图图一起成立了真相与和解委员会，以调查种族主义时代白人和黑人所犯下的罪行

vocabulary.context: The interest manifested by all classes was not so much for the legal principles involved, as for the mute instincts that mold every human heart - the undying love of freedom that is planted in every breast - the resolve to die rather than submit to a life of degradation and bondage.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 所有阶层所表现出的兴趣与其说是对所涉及的法律原则，不如说是对塑造每个人心的无声本能--每个人心中都植根着对自由的不朽的热爱--决心去死，而不是屈服于堕落和奴役的生活

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 领带乡村，穿着马皮靴，在门口等候；从书本中诞生的救赎者不明白，当救世主的声音释放了地球的灵魂时，一场革命就取得了胜利，它必须与地球的灵魂一起统治，而不是反对或没有它。 【08】电视新观点

vocabulary.context: After singing "Joe Hill," the famous ballad about a Swedish-born union activist falsely accused and convicted of murder and executed in Utah in 1915, Robeson addressed the audience and began speaking extemporaneously, as he often did, about the lives of black people in the United States.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 唱完这首著名民谣《乔·希尔》（Joe Hill），讲述了一位瑞典出生的工会活动家被错误指控并被定罪谋杀，并于 1915 年在犹他州被处决的故事，罗伯逊向观众发表讲话，并开始即兴讲述美国黑人的生活，就像他经常做的那样

vocabulary.definition: n. 废止, 取消, 撤回；[经] 撤销

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他被列入黑名单并被吊销护照，使他无法工作或旅行，他的年收入从超过 15 万美元降至不到 3,000 美元

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他在新泽西州普林斯顿长大，在那里他作为有史以来最伟大的橄榄球运动员之一而闻名，并于 1917 年和 1918 年在罗格斯大学连续两年入选全美最佳阵容第一队

vocabulary.definition: n. 常参加音乐会者( concertgoer的名词复数 )
vocabulary.context: Later that summer, in civil rights-friendly Westchester County, New York, at the one concert that was not canceled, anti-communist groups and Ku Klux Klan types hurled racial epithets, attacked concertgoers with baseball bats and rocks and burned Robeson in effigy.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 那年夏天晚些时候，在支持民权的纽约州韦斯特切斯特县，在一场没有被取消的音乐会上，反共团体和三K党成员投掷种族绰号，用棒球棒和石块袭击音乐会观众，并烧毁了罗伯逊的肖像

vocabulary.definition: v. 错误地引用

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 历史学家后来发现罗伯逊被错误引用，但损害几乎是立即造成的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 全国有色人种协进会始终警惕与共产主义者有任何联系，因此与罗伯逊划清了界限

vocabulary.context: By the following day, editorialists and politicians had branded Robeson a communist traitor for insinuating that black Americans would not fight in a war against the Soviet Union.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 第二天，社论家和政界人士将罗伯逊称为共产主义叛徒，因为他暗示美国黑人不会参加针对苏联的战争

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 杰基·罗宾逊的直率言论对他们帮助很大。"对于罗伯森来说，批评是尖锐的，尤其是来自棒球明星的批评

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 擦去, 冲刷(掉), 消灭, 忘掉, 盖销(邮票等)；[法] 灭迹, 消灭, 涂抹
vocabulary.context: Channing Tobias, a member of the NAACP board of directors, called him "an ingrate." Three months later, on July 18, 1949, Jackie Robinson was brought to Washington, D.C., to testify before HUAC for the purpose of obliterating Robeson's leadership role in the American black community.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 全国有色人种协进会董事会成员查宁·托比亚斯称他为"忘恩负义的人"。三个月后，即 1949 年 7 月 18 日，杰基·罗宾逊被带到华盛顿特区，在 HUAC 面前作证，目的是抹杀罗伯逊在美国黑人社区的领导作用

vocabulary.definition: n. 认定为私生子；[法] 认定为非婚生子
vocabulary.context: No name is safe from future bastardization or shape-shifting.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有一个名字可以免受未来的私生化或变形

vocabulary.definition: n. 重, 沉重, 重要性
vocabulary.context: relating to the ear or sense of hearing constantly changing with the era, stockpiling references, nuance, and weightiness in society's mental bunker.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与随着时代不断变化的耳朵或听觉有关，在社会的精神掩体中储存参考资料、细微差别和分量

vocabulary.definition: n. 决定论；[化] 决定论
vocabulary.context: This belief - backed by lots of anecdotes and some research - spawned the theories of nominative determinism (the idea that one's name directly impacts what he or she does in life) and aptronym (the notion that a name is often aptly suited to its owner).
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种信念--得到大量轶事和一些研究的支持--催生了主格决定论（一个人的名字直接影响他或她在生活中所做的事情的观点）和aptronym（一个名字通常非常适合其所有者的观点）的理论

vocabulary.definition: adj. 未磨光的, 无光泽的, 未经润饰的
vocabulary.context: Writers are largely preoccupied with words, rolling them around like unpolished rocks in our minds and on the page until smooth, glistening sentences emerge.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作家主要专注于文字，将它们像未经打磨的岩石一样在我们的脑海中和纸面上滚动，直到出现流畅、闪闪发光的句子

vocabulary.definition: adj. 圆桌的, 圆台
vocabulary.context: There's no mental ping-pong over what we'd like to be called happening in utero, no roundtable discussion with fellow crying newborns in the nursery about whether we should be called "Arthur" or "Arlo." I've been deeply fascinated with names since I was a child - their cadence and candor, how they flit off of the tongue - and how powerful they can be.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 没有关于我们希望被称为子宫内发生的事情的心理乒乓球，没有与托儿所里哭泣的新生儿一起讨论我们是否应该被称为"亚瑟"或"阿洛"。我从小就对名字深深着迷--它们的节奏和坦率，它们如何从舌头上飞过--以及它们的力量有多大

vocabulary.definition: v.t. 厚厚地涂, 大量使用；n. 大量
vocabulary.context: My low point arrived when I had a heated internal debate over whether or not a tapenade could be "slathered." "Untitled" by Garrett Jackson is licensed under Despite being control-freak wordsmiths, CC0.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当我内部就是否可以"涂上厚厚的一层橄榄酱"进行激烈的争论时，我的人生最低谷到来了。加勒特·杰克逊 (Garrett Jackson) 的《无题》获得 CC0 许可，尽管他是控制狂文字大师

vocabulary.definition: adj. 耳的, 听觉的, 气氛的, 气味的, 光环的；[医] 耳的; 先兆的
vocabulary.context: It's not unusual to witness an intense discussion about whether "Daphne Jane" or "Daphne June" has a better aural flow.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 关于"Daphne Jane"或"Daphne June"是否具有更好的听觉流畅性的激烈讨论并不罕见

vocabulary.definition: adj. 诡诈的, 突兀的, 古怪的, 离奇的；[法] 诡诈的, 颇为曲折的
vocabulary.context: Such a quirky interest isn't something that's easy to chatter about at slumber parties or sock hops, and certainly wasn't a way to make my fellow middle schoolers think I was less of an odd duck.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 这种古怪的兴趣并不是一件容易在睡衣派对或袜子舞会上谈论的事情，当然也不会让我的中学生同学认为我不那么奇怪

vocabulary.definition: n. 跳跃；v.i. 雀跃, 蹦跳

vocabulary.definition: adj. 精神分析的；[医] 精神分析的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 他同事的名字（以及他自己的名字）从德语翻译过来时，通常反映了他们的精神分析风格

vocabulary.definition: n. 国际主义, 国际性；[经] 国际主义
vocabulary.context: By attempting to inoculate the whole of the Soviet intelligentsia against Western thought and theory the party theorists are tackling an unprecedented task and one that runs counter to the internationalism and universalism of basic Communist doctrine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过试图向整个苏联知识界灌输西方思想和理论的思想，党的理论家们正在应对一项前所未有的任务，这项任务与共产主义基本学说的国际主义和普遍主义背道而驰

vocabulary.definition: n. 教导, 教化；[法] 教育, 教训, 灌输
vocabulary.context: For the West there is substantial encouragement to be drawn from the fact that after thirty years of Marxian indoctrination and party propaganda the Kremlin still finds it necessary to launch a "thought purge" of the scope and character now under way.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 对于西方来说，经过三十年的马克思主义灌输和政党宣传，克里姆林宫仍然认为有必要对目前正在进行的范围和性质进行"思想清洗"，这一事实令人鼓舞

vocabulary.definition: n. 使成为受救济过活的贫民, 贫穷化
vocabulary.context: As Pravda put it, acceptance of Varga's theories would lead one to believe "that the bourgeois state is capable of overcoming the spontaneous action of the law of value, the general law of capitalist accumulation and the law of the pauperization of the working class; that it can overcome depressions and unemployment-that it can become planned."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 正如《真理报》所说，接受瓦尔加的理论会让人们相信"资产阶级国家有能力克服价值规律的自发行动、资本主义积累的一般规律和工人阶级的贫困化规律；它可以克服萧条和失业--它可以成为有计划的。"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 资本主义的, 资本家的
vocabulary.context: EUGEN VARGA-for challenging the Soviet dogmas of inevitable capitalistic collapse.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尤根·瓦尔加（EUGEN VARGA）--挑战苏联资本主义不可避免崩溃的教条

vocabulary.definition: n. 普救论, 普遍性, 普遍特征
vocabulary.context: By attempting to inoculate the whole of the Soviet intelligentsia against Western thought and theory the party theorists are tackling an unprecedented task and one that runs counter to the internationalism and universalism of basic Communist doctrine.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 通过试图向整个苏联知识界灌输西方思想和理论的思想，党的理论家们正在应对一项前所未有的任务，这项任务与共产主义基本学说的国际主义和普遍主义背道而驰

vocabulary.definition: n. 无端畏惧和憎恨外国人/陌生人, 对外国人的畏惧和憎恨；[医] 生客恐怖
vocabulary.context: Nationalism and xenophobia are the main components of the Soviet criticism rather than dialectic considerations.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 民族主义和仇外心理是对苏联批评的主要成分，而不是辩证的考虑

vocabulary.definition: n. 惩罚, 苛评, 修订
vocabulary.context: In other words, the common theme of these criticisms is frank nationalism which is only thinly veiled by ideological castigation of Western models as "bourgeois" or "decadent." It is nationalism of an extreme type, as can be seen by Pravda's comment in the case of Zhebrak.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 换句话说，这些批评的共同主题是坦率的民族主义，而这种民族主义只是在意识形态上对西方模式进行"资产阶级"或"颓废"的谴责。这是一种极端的民族主义，从《真理报》对热布拉克事件的评论中可以看出

vocabulary.definition: n. 外国（人）的特性
vocabulary.context: Parallel to this nationalism is the growing tendency to denounce foreign objects and influences, frequently for no more basic reason than their foreignness.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 与这种民族主义并行的是谴责外来事物和影响的日益增长的趋势，其原因往往只是其外来性

vocabulary.definition: adj. 形式主义的
vocabulary.context: DIRECTORS OF THE BOLSHOI THEATRE-for "encouraging decadent, formalistic music."
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 莫斯科大剧院总监 - "鼓励颓废、形式主义的音乐"

vocabulary.definition: adj. 不公正的, 邪恶的
vocabulary.context: While the Moscow press thunders against Western diplomacy and denounces Western "warmongers," while the ordinary Soviet citizen is barred from all contact with foreigners and is drenched by daily press and radio warnings against the iniquitous West, the Soviet intellectual is being whipped into line by a more complex technique.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当莫斯科媒体猛烈抨击西方外交并谴责西方"战争贩子"时，当普通苏联公民被禁止与外国人进行任何接触，并每天接受针对邪恶西方的新闻和广播警告时，苏联知识分子正在被一种更复杂的手段鞭打

vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...被以十人为一组，用绳子绑起来，然后推入乱葬坑，要么用泥土覆盖，要么被烧毁； • 被俘虏的中国士兵被剖腹并吃他们的烤心和肝； • 三分之一的城市被烧毁，抢劫和盗窃现象普遍存在

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "应征入伍的陆军和海军"由强制征召入伍的士兵组成，这意味着他们在这件事上别无选择

vocabulary.definition: n. 结语, 尾声, 收场白；[计] 收尾程序

vocabulary.definition: n. 封地, 采邑（等于fief）

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 封建制度的核心是封建制度，在这种制度下，人们被授予一块土地或"封地"，人们可以在土地上工作，以换取向拥有土地的领主提供的服务或费用

vocabulary.definition: n. 恶意；邪恶
vocabulary.context: The perception of an entire nation as disgusting, subhuman beings fit for slaughter explains the viciousness of Japan's pursuit of its war aims, which were to destroy the Chinese republic and replace it with subservient "Manchukuos"-provinces run by Quislings -who would supply Japan with the needed food and natural resources.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 将整个国家视为令人厌恶的、适合屠杀的低等人，这解释了日本追求其战争目标的邪恶性，即摧毁中华民国并用屈从的"满洲国"（由吉斯林统治的省份）取而代之，后者将为日本提供所需的粮食和自然资源。 【05】最完美的，我留给你

vocabulary.definition: adj. 警告的

Chinese casualties in...
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...从南京事件中汲取的教训，其中之一是文明本身是薄弱的...南京大屠杀应该被视为一个警示故事 - 它说明了人类是多么容易被鼓励让他们的青少年被塑造成高效的杀人机器，从而抑制他们更好的本性。"

vocabulary.contextTranslation: "我们知道法院不会解决所有问题，"柬埔寨文献中心创始人 Youk Chhang 说，该组织收集了红色高棉时代的大量文件和照片

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 周四在柬埔寨首都金边郊区的一家法院进行的审判主要集中在城市中心的疏散问题，这是红色高棉建立农业乌托邦的灾难性尝试的一部分

vocabulary.definition: n. outlandish的变形
vocabulary.context: In fact, it may be that what is remarkable about Shakespeare's language is not its outlandishness but how close it is to the overall standard of the language of his time, as another numbers-based analysis suggests.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 事实上，正如另一项基于数字的分析所表明的那样，莎士比亚语言的非凡之处可能并不在于它的古怪，而在于它与他那个时代语言的总体标准有多么接近

vocabulary.definition: n. 语文学者, 文献学者
vocabulary.context: Looking further back, a century and a half ago the philologist Max Müller contrasted the 300 words used by a rural labourer with the 3,000 of the educated person of his day and the 15,000 of Shakespeare's.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 进一步回顾，一个半世纪前，语言学家马克斯·穆勒将农村劳动者使用的 300 个单词与同时代受过教育的人使用的 3,000 个单词以及莎士比亚使用的 15,000 个单词进行了对比

vocabulary.definition: n. 文体学
vocabulary.context: But in an age of data claims such as these are bound to be tested, and two separate studies, one in a book on stylistics (2011), and one in a Shakespeare journal (also from 2011), have now shown that, when you compare like with like, Shakespeare does not in fact have a very large vocabulary.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 但在数据时代，诸如此类的说法必然会受到检验，两项独立的研究，一项是在一本关于文体学的书中（2011年），另一项是在莎士比亚期刊（也是2011年）中，现在表明，当你比较同类时，莎士比亚实际上并没有很大的词汇量

vocabulary.definition: adj. 卓越的; 优秀的; 杰出的; 显著的

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 西方传统中杰出的创意作家应该拥有迄今为止已知的最大的词汇量，这似乎是很自然的事情1

vocabulary.definition: n. 文明, 文明世界, 文化, 开化, 教化
vocabulary.context: This may work well when the writer wants to depict an unfamiliar world like the civilisation of a remote planet (science fiction) or an underground, secret organisation (gangster fiction) but not with a domestic comedy, or an imaginary dialogue between two people who know each other well.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当作者想要描绘一个陌生的世界，例如遥远星球的文明（科幻小说）或地下秘密组织（黑帮小说），而不是家庭喜剧或两个彼此熟悉的人之间的想象对话时，这可能会很有效

vocabulary.context: ...made to preserve his plays after his death; and the fact that whereas there are many good ways of estimating his vocabulary from concordances and good complete editions, the same was not true for his peers.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: ...为了在他死后保存他的戏剧而制作；事实上，虽然有很多好的方法可以根据索引和良好的完整版本来估计他的词汇量，但对于他的同龄人来说却并非如此

vocabulary.definition: n. 组织, 团体, 体制, 编制

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 当作者想要描绘一个陌生的世界，例如遥远星球的文明（科幻小说）或地下秘密组织（黑帮小说），而不是家庭喜剧或两个彼此熟悉的人之间的想象对话时，这可能会很有效

vocabulary.definition: n. 庞然大物, 非凡的人, 闹饮, 驯马人, 小家伙, 小鬼, 炸弹, 巨型炸弹
vocabulary.context: Although, as one of the busters of the Shakespeare vocabulary myth Ward Elliott pointed out to me, it's not fair to put the Wu-Tang Clan on the same scale as individual rappers, as combining different writers will always make for a larger overall vocabulary.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 尽管，正如莎士比亚词汇神话的破坏者之一沃德·埃利奥特（Ward Elliott）向我指出的那样，将武当派与个别说唱歌手放在同一规模上是不公平的，因为结合不同的作家总是会产生更大的整体词汇量

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 为了表彰和庆祝这些人员对学生生活做出的贡献，乔治城大学校友 Febin Bellamy 于 2016 年创立了无名英雄组织

vocabulary.definition: adj. 回答的, 应答的, 反射的；n. 应答者, 被告
vocabulary.context: Following up on a 2014 Gallup-Purdue national survey, the Elon Poll found that more than 80 percent of respondents reported their most important faculty or staff relationship formed in college was with someone who made them excited about learning, cared about them as a person and encouraged them to pursue their dreams.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 继 2014 年盖洛普-普渡大学全国调查之后，埃隆民意调查发现，超过 80% 的受访者表示，他们在大学中形成的最重要的教职员工关系是与那些让他们对学习感到兴奋、关心他们并鼓励他们追求梦想的人

vocabulary.definition: n. mentoring是一种工作关系。mentor通常是处在比mentee更高工作职位上的有影响力的人。他/她有比‘mentee’更丰富的工作经验和知识, 并用心支持mentee的职业（发展）。；v. （无经验之人的）有经验可信赖的顾问( mentor的现在分词 )
vocabulary.context: These are points two of us plan to explore more deeply as co-authors of a forthcoming book on mentoring in college.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 作为即将出版的一本关于大学辅导的书的合著者，我们两人计划更深入地探讨这些要点

vocabulary.definition: n. 奥德赛(古希腊史诗)

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 另一位是本科教育学者

vocabulary.definition: n. 付薪水的支票, 薪水

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 例如，通过将每份薪水的一部分存入您的支出计划中，您就可以在很小的时候就养成良好的财务习惯

vocabulary.definition: adj. 有害的
vocabulary.context: a single payment made at a particular time, as opposed to a number of smaller payments made over time A spending plan with emergency savings can also make sudden and unforeseen circumstances less financially detrimental.
vocabulary.contextTranslation: 在特定时间支付的单笔款项，而不是在一段时间内支付的多次小额款项 包含紧急储蓄的支出计划还可以减少突然和不可预见的情况造成的财务损失

vocabulary.definition: adv. 有害地; 淘气地

"Have you ever seen, round the vomitories, the bones of those who were extruded after the Great Rebellion?" "Yes." "They were left where they perished for our edification.

vocabulary.definition: n. 主题, 素材

vocabulary.definition: n. 弱, 微弱

vocabulary.context: And, then and there, I threw myself hurriedly at the feet of Eleonora, and offered up a vow, to herself and to Heaven, that I would never bind myself in marriage to any daughter of Earth- that I would in no manner prove recreant to her dear memory, or to the memory of the devout affection with which she had blessed me.

vocabulary.definition: n. 蜉蝣

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 她已经看到死亡的手指在她的胸前--就像蜉蝣一样，她在美丽中变得完美，只是为了死去；但对她来说，坟墓的恐惧仅在于她在一个黄昏时分，在寂静之河岸边向我透露的一种考虑

vocabulary.contextTranslation: 最后，有一天，她流着泪谈到了人类必将面临的最后一个悲伤的变化，从那时起，她只专注于这个悲伤的主题，将其交织到我们所有的27-28对话中，就像在施拉兹吟游诗人的歌曲中一样，在每一个令人印象深刻的短语变体中，都发现同样的图像一次又一次地出现
